message:
feat(cli,ui): wire daemon client, add analytics-aware filters, and expand arg normalization

Three interrelated changes that improve daemon integration, fix surface-aware
UI behavior, and keep the CLI argument normalizer current.

1. Daemon client integration (src/lib.rs):
   Previously, both the embedder and reranker code paths unconditionally created
   a NoopDaemonClient when --daemon was requested, meaning the daemon flag was
   effectively a no-op. Now the code actually attempts to connect to a running
   daemon via `crate::daemon::client::try_connect()` on Unix, falling back to
   NoopDaemonClient only when no daemon is available. Non-Unix platforms retain
   the noop behavior behind #[cfg(not(unix))]. This makes semantic search with
   a daemon process genuinely functional rather than silently degraded.

2. Analytics surface awareness in UI filters (src/ui/app.rs):
   Filter messages (FilterAgentSet, FilterWorkspaceSet, FilterTimeSet,
   FiltersClearAll) and input mode application (InputModeApplied) previously
   always dispatched SearchRequested, which is wrong when the active surface is
   Analytics — it would trigger a search reload instead of updating the
   analytics view. These handlers now check `self.surface == AppSurface::Analytics`
   and dispatch the corresponding Analytics* messages (AnalyticsAgentFilterSet,
   AnalyticsWorkspaceFilterSet, AnalyticsTimeRangeSet, AnalyticsFiltersClearAll)
   when appropriate.

   Similarly, `set_loading_context(LoadingContext::Search)` is now gated behind
   a surface check so the analytics surface does not show a misleading "search
   loading" indicator. The startup init path and state-load-completed handler
   also branch on surface to call `schedule_analytics_reload()` instead of
   unconditionally requesting a search.

3. Resize-triggered search and analytics cache invalidation (src/ui/app.rs):
   Terminal resize events now track breakpoint changes via LayoutBreakpoint and
   invalidate the analytics cache when the layout breakpoint crosses a threshold,
   preventing stale analytics renders after significant width changes. Vertical
   growth also triggers SearchRequested so panes can fetch enough hits to fill
   newly available screen real estate.

4. Pane resize triggers search (src/ui/app.rs):
   PaneGrown and PaneShrunk handlers previously returned Cmd::none(), meaning
   pane size changes were purely cosmetic — the hit count would not adjust to
   fill or trim the resized pane. They now dispatch SearchRequested so the
   per-pane limit takes effect immediately.

5. Index refresh reloads current surface (src/ui/app.rs):
   After a successful index refresh, the handler now reloads the active surface
   (analytics or search) instead of doing nothing, so freshly indexed data is
   immediately visible without a manual refresh.

6. Argument normalization expansion (src/lib.rs):
   Added "yesterday" to date-related flags and "watch-interval" to watch flags.
   Added 25 new flags to the normalization list (stale-threshold, by-source,
   robot-meta, robot-format, max-content-length, fast-only, quality-only, once,
   reset-state, asciicast, inline, ui-height, anchor, record-macro, play-macro,
   group-by, mirror, from-file, repair, purge, preset, no-test, no-index,
   skip-existing, hosts) to keep the normalizer in sync with recently added CLI
   capabilities.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
